description: K0-K44 DDGC Monster And Encounter Micro-Slices — decompose enemy migration into very small, family-level steps so Claude can migrate one registry unit, one monster family, or one encounter wiring slice at a time.

userStories.title: K0: Lock the monster family inventory baseline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the DDGC monster inventory frozen as the migration source of truth so every later step can target an explicit family instead of a vague enemy bucket.
acceptanceCriteria: games/game_ddgc_headless/MONSTER_FAMILIES.md exists
The inventory distinguishes common families, bosses, and boss-only parts or minions
The inventory is sufficient to drive one-family-per-step migration
userStories.notes: Already complete. This stays as the source document for all micro-slices below.

userStories.title: K1: Add monster registry schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a MonsterFamily registry schema so each future story can register exactly one DDGC family with typed role, tier, dungeon, and skill-pack metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: games/game_ddgc_headless/src/monsters/families.rs exists
The registry exposes family id, dungeon, tier, role, archetype builder, and skill ids
The registry explicitly models DDGC common and boss tiers without inventing a fake elite tier
userStories.notes: Keep the type system minimal and DDGC-faithful.

userStories.title: K2: Add monster registry smoke tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need registry smoke tests so structural drift is caught before any single family migration starts piling on top of a broken lookup layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests monster_family_registry_builds, monster_family_registry_exposes_dungeon_tags, and monster_family_registry_exposes_skill_ids exist
The tests compile against the new registry API
cargo test --manifest-path games/game_ddgc_headless/Cargo.toml monster_family_registry exits 0
userStories.notes: This should be a tiny structural slice before content migration.

userStories.title: K3: Wire monster content module entrypoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the monster content module wired into the crate so later one-family slices can add new files without touching unrelated systems each time.
acceptanceCriteria: games/game_ddgc_headless/src/content/monsters/ directory exists
Monster registry builders can resolve family-local content modules
The crate typechecks after introducing the monster content entrypoints
userStories.notes: This is plumbing only; do not migrate multiple families here.

userStories.title: K4: Migrate mantis_magic_flower family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need mantis_magic_flower migrated as its own family slice so poison plus crowd-bleed semantics are preserved independently of the other mantis variants.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for mantis_magic_flower
The registry can resolve mantis_magic_flower
A focused test proves poison plus crowd-bleed identity is preserved

userStories.title: K5: Migrate mantis_spiny_flower family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need mantis_spiny_flower migrated separately so armor-piercing blight behavior does not get flattened into a generic mantis template.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for mantis_spiny_flower
The registry can resolve mantis_spiny_flower
A focused test proves ignore-armor plus crowd-bleed identity is preserved

userStories.title: K6: Migrate mantis_walking_flower family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need mantis_walking_flower migrated separately so weak-plus-bleed debuff identity survives without being merged with the other mantis records.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for mantis_walking_flower
The registry can resolve mantis_walking_flower
A focused test proves weak plus crowd-bleed identity is preserved

userStories.title: K7: Migrate dry_tree_genie family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need dry_tree_genie migrated as its own slice so ranged bleed, slow crowd pressure, and stress identity can be validated directly.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for dry_tree_genie
The registry can resolve dry_tree_genie
A focused test proves bleed, slow-crowd, and stress identity is preserved

userStories.description: As a developer, I need moth_mimicry_A migrated independently so its ranged blight profile is not conflated with the other moth branch.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for moth_mimicry_A
The registry can resolve moth_mimicry_A
A focused test proves poison plus stress-poison identity is preserved

userStories.description: As a developer, I need moth_mimicry_B migrated independently so AoE stress behavior is not flattened into the same profile as moth_mimicry_A.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for moth_mimicry_B
The registry can resolve moth_mimicry_B
A focused test proves poison plus crowd-stress identity is preserved

userStories.description: As a developer, I need robber_melee migrated independently so the fragile melee-stun-debuff profile can be tested without being lost inside a generic robber bucket.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for robber_melee
The registry can resolve robber_melee
A focused test proves low-HP melee control identity is preserved

acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for robber_ranged

A focused test proves low-HP ranged stun identity is preserved

acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for metal_armor

A focused test proves high-prot stun-plus-bleed identity is preserved

A focused test proves fast control plus stress identity is preserved

userStories.description: As a developer, I need unicorn_beetle_A migrated independently so ranged bleed pressure is preserved separately from the stress-oriented beetle variant.

The registry can resolve unicorn_beetle_A
A focused test proves ranged bleed identity is preserved

acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for unicorn_beetle_B
The registry can resolve unicorn_beetle_B
A focused test proves ranged bleed plus stress identity is preserved

userStories.description: As a developer, I need alligator_yangtze migrated independently so bruiser plus riposte semantics are preserved as their own family identity.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for alligator_yangtze
The registry can resolve alligator_yangtze
A focused test proves bruiser plus riposte identity is preserved

acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for ghost_fire_assist
The registry can resolve ghost_fire_assist
A focused test proves assist plus split-on-death identity is preserved

userStories.description: As a developer, I need ghost_fire_damage migrated independently so burn plus stress plus split-on-death behavior is preserved separately from the support variant.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for ghost_fire_damage
The registry can resolve ghost_fire_damage
A focused test proves burn plus stress plus split-on-death identity is preserved

userStories.title: K20: Migrate fox_fire family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need fox_fire migrated independently so bleed, burn-debuff vomit, and ally-guard semantics remain a distinct defensive bruiser profile.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for fox_fire
The registry can resolve fox_fire
A focused test proves bleed plus burn debuff plus guard identity is preserved

userStories.title: K21: Migrate moth_fire family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need moth_fire migrated independently so stress attack, cocoon defense-heal cycle, and fire dive behavior survive as one coherent family.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for moth_fire
The registry can resolve moth_fire
A focused test proves cocoon cycle plus fire pressure identity is preserved

userStories.description: As a developer, I need lantern migrated independently so magic burn plus stress pressure is preserved as its own ranged threat profile.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for lantern
The registry can resolve lantern
A focused test proves ranged burn plus stress identity is preserved

userStories.title: K23: Migrate snake_water family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need snake_water migrated independently so stun plus poison-fang identity remains a clear water-dungeon control family.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for snake_water
The registry can resolve snake_water
A focused test proves stun plus blight identity is preserved

userStories.title: K24: Migrate water_grass family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need water_grass migrated independently so its five-skill control kit does not get flattened into a generic plant enemy.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for water_grass
The registry can resolve water_grass
A focused test proves stun, bleed, crowd attack, and pull identity is preserved

userStories.title: K25: Migrate monkey_water family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need monkey_water migrated independently so charge, stress-tag melee, and bruiser behavior are preserved as their own family identity.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated monster content definition exists for monkey_water
The registry can resolve monkey_water
A focused test proves rush plus stress identity is preserved

userStories.title: K26: Add common monster family parity fixtures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a parity layer for the migrated common families so the first 22 content slices are protected by reusable assertions instead of ad hoc tests only.
acceptanceCriteria: games/game_ddgc_headless/src/parity/monster_families.rs exists
games/game_ddgc_headless/tests/monster_family_semantic_parity.rs exists
The parity layer covers at least one identity assertion for every migrated common family
userStories.notes: This is the first consolidation step after all common families exist.

userStories.title: K27: Build common encounter pack registry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need common encounter packs grouped by dungeon so run flow can consume real DDGC enemy groups instead of one-off test fights.
acceptanceCriteria: games/game_ddgc_headless/src/encounters/mod.rs exists
games/game_ddgc_headless/src/encounters/packs.rs exists
At least one common encounter pack exists for QingLong, BaiHu, ZhuQue, and XuanWu
userStories.notes: Keep this limited to common encounters only.

userStories.title: K28: Integrate common encounter packs into combat rooms
userStories.description: As a developer, I need combat rooms to resolve through the common encounter registry so the progression layer starts consuming migrated DDGC families.
acceptanceCriteria: games/game_ddgc_headless/src/run/encounters.rs exists
Combat rooms resolve to common encounter packs rather than a single hard-coded battle
A test proves room-to-common-encounter mapping is deterministic
userStories.notes: Do not wire boss rooms yet.

userStories.title: K29: Migrate azure_dragon boss family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need azure_dragon migrated as its own boss slice so shared-health ball mechanics and storm-control behavior are preserved together.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for azure_dragon
Required boss-part units for azure_dragon are modeled or stubbed through the encounter layer
A focused test proves shared-health plus summon-ball semantics are preserved

userStories.title: K30: Migrate vermilion_bird boss family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need vermilion_bird migrated as its own boss slice so shared-health tail parts and burn-heal cycle semantics are preserved together.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for vermilion_bird
Required tail-part units for vermilion_bird are modeled or stubbed through the encounter layer
A focused test proves shared-health plus burn-heal cycle semantics are preserved

userStories.title: K31: Migrate white_tiger boss family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need white_tiger migrated as its own boss slice so clone-phase and three-turn final-form semantics are preserved instead of collapsed into one stat block.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for white_tiger_C or equivalent family root
Required clone and terrain units for white_tiger are modeled or stubbed through the encounter layer
A focused test proves multi-phase turn-economy semantics are preserved

userStories.title: K32: Migrate black_tortoise boss family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need black_tortoise migrated as one composite boss slice so twin-body coordination and shared-damage behavior stay coherent across turtle and snake bodies.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for the black_tortoise composite family
Both A and B bodies are represented through the encounter layer
A focused test proves dual-body coordination and shared-damage semantics are preserved

acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for rotvine_wraith
Required rotten_fruit units are modeled or stubbed through the encounter layer
A focused test proves summon-fruit plus control semantics are preserved

acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for skeletal_tiller
The vegetable summon unit is modeled or stubbed through the encounter layer
A focused test proves summon plus frozen-stress identity is preserved

acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for necrodrake_embryosac
The egg_membrane capture units are modeled or stubbed through the encounter layer
A focused test proves capture-and-release semantics are preserved

acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for gambler
The mahjong summon units are modeled or stubbed through the encounter layer
A focused test proves summon plus random-pressure identity is preserved

userStories.title: K37: Migrate scorchthroat_chanteuse boss family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need scorchthroat_chanteuse migrated as its own boss slice so summon-instrument and burn-magic pressure are preserved together.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for scorchthroat_chanteuse
The instrument summon units are modeled or stubbed through the encounter layer
A focused test proves summon plus burn-stress identity is preserved

userStories.title: K38: Migrate frostvein_clam boss family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need frostvein_clam migrated as its own boss slice so riposte and pearlkin summon semantics are preserved together.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for frostvein_clam
The pearlkin summon units are modeled or stubbed through the encounter layer
A focused test proves riposte plus summon semantics are preserved

userStories.title: K39: Migrate bloodthirsty_assassin boss family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need bloodthirsty_assassin migrated as its own boss slice so paired-shadow and HP-averaging semantics remain intact.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for bloodthirsty_assassin
The paired shadow unit is modeled or stubbed through the encounter layer
A focused test proves paired-boss and HP-averaging semantics are preserved

userStories.title: K40: Migrate glutton_pawnshop boss family
userStories.description: As a developer, I need glutton_pawnshop migrated as its own boss slice so controller mechanics and tag-driven debuff logic stay intact.
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated boss content definition exists for glutton_pawnshop
Controller and tag-based effects are represented or explicitly ledgered as game-gap work
A focused test proves controller-plus-tag semantics are preserved or consciously ledgered

userStories.title: K41: Add boss encounter parity and golden traces
userStories.description: As a developer, I need boss-specific parity fixtures and traces so future changes cannot silently flatten shared-health, summon, multi-body, or control mechanics.
acceptanceCriteria: games/game_ddgc_headless/tests/encounter_trace.rs exists
games/game_ddgc_headless/fixtures/encounters/ directory exists
At least one trace exists for a shared-health boss, a summon boss, and a paired or multi-body boss
userStories.notes: This is the boss-side consolidation step.

userStories.title: K42: Integrate boss encounter packs into boss rooms
userStories.description: As a developer, I need boss rooms to resolve through real DDGC boss encounter packs so run flow stops treating bosses as generic combat rooms with bigger rewards.
acceptanceCriteria: Boss rooms resolve to migrated DDGC boss encounter packs
A test proves boss-room mapping is deterministic and uses the encounter registry
cargo run --manifest-path games/game_ddgc_headless/Cargo.toml exercises the new boss-room path without crashing
userStories.notes: Wire boss rooms only after at least one boss encounter path exists.

userStories.title: K43: Replace placeholder enemies in gameplay entrypoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need gameplay entrypoints to stop depending on Bone Soldier and Necromancer placeholders once real DDGC common and boss packs exist.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary gameplay entrypoints no longer rely on placeholder Bone Soldier or Necromancer content for migrated rooms
Any remaining placeholder use is isolated to legacy tests or explicitly marked transitional
A focused test proves migrated encounter content is now the default path
userStories.notes: This is the cleanup slice before final verification.

userStories.title: K44: Deliver the enemy-side migration baseline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the enemy-side baseline closed out so DDGC headless is no longer hero-complete but enemy-placeholder driven.
acceptanceCriteria: Common monster families are registered through the family registry
Encounter packs drive combat rooms and at least one boss-room path
cargo test --manifest-path games/game_ddgc_headless/Cargo.toml exits 0
cargo run --manifest-path games/game_ddgc_headless/Cargo.toml exits 0
userStories.notes: Close the phase only after the micro-slices above have converged.
